app.title: Windows Care Kit
app.tagline: Uninstall cleanly · clean up · back up before you format · reinstall
nav.migration.desc: Choose what moves to the new PC
uninstall.title: Installed programs
uninstall.subtitle: Pick a program to preview its plan. Nothing changes until you approve the dry-run.
uninstall.selectPrompt: Select a program on the left to see its uninstall plan and leftover scan.
uninstall.official.title: Official uninstaller
uninstall.official.none: No uninstall string is registered for this program.
uninstall.leftovers.title: Leftover scan (dry-run)
uninstall.readonlyNotice: Every change is shown as a dry-run and runs only after you approve it.
uninstall.confirm.title: Confirm — this will make changes
uninstall.confirm.body: These are the exact actions that will run. Deleted files go to the Recycle Bin and a .reg backup is taken before each registry change, where possible.
uninstall.result.title: Result
uninstall.appx.irreversible: Store app removal can't be undone.
uninstall.col.name: Name
uninstall.wizard.prep.title: Set up the rollback layers before deleting
uninstall.wizard.prep.subtitle: This step changes nothing permanently. The official uninstaller runs through the ConfirmGate after you approve.
uninstall.wizard.prep.restorePoint.title: Create a System Restore point
uninstall.wizard.prep.restorePoint.reaso: ⚠ Needs admin, or System Restore is off — unavailable on this machine.
Created just before the official uninstaller, as part of the plan you approve.
uninstall.wizard.restorePoint.name: Windows Care Kit — before uninstalling {0}
uninstall.wizard.prep.honest: A .reg backup is taken before each registry delete (adapter-internal, not a separate toggle) + files are moved to the Recycle Bin. These do not depend on System Restore.
uninstall.wizard.official.unavailable: 🔧 Uninstaller broken — this step is skipped; go straight to the scan.
uninstall.wizard.scan.title: Leftover scan
uninstall.wizard.scan.subtitle: The scan checks the app's known locations and publisher folders. Protected items always stay protected.
uninstall.wizard.depth.quick.desc: In this version the options run the same scan.
uninstall.wizard.depth.standard.desc: In this version the options run the same scan.
uninstall.wizard.depth.deep.desc: Not yet differentiated — real depth lands in a later slice.
uninstall.wizard.leftovers.title: Leftover items
uninstall.wizard.leftovers.subtitle: Only bold (program-owned) rows are selectable and default unchecked. Shared/parent rows are shown for context — they cannot be deleted.
uninstall.wizard.helper: Press "Delete selected" to delete. "Next" alone deletes nothing.
uninstall.wizard.buildError: Could not build the delete plan: a shared/protected item ({0}) cannot enter the plan. Nothing was deleted — review your selection.
uninstall.wizard.sub.owned: Created by {0} · a .reg backup is taken
uninstall.wizard.sub.shared: shared/context — could affect other software · cannot be deleted (shown for orientation)
uninstall.wizard.sub.protected: {0} · never added to the delete list
uninstall.wizard.confirm.official.body: The vendor's official uninstaller runs as below.
uninstall.wizard.confirm.leftovers.body: The program-owned leftovers you selected will be deleted. A .reg backup is taken before each registry delete; files go to the Recycle Bin.
uninstall.wizard.status.officialStaged: The official uninstaller is awaiting confirmation.
uninstall.wizard.status.officialRunning: the official uninstaller is running… — wait until it finishes (cannot be interrupted).
uninstall.wizard.status.leftoversStaged: The selected leftovers are awaiting confirmation.
uninstall.wizard.result.title: Done — {0} processed
uninstall.wizard.result.restoreNote: System Restore was unavailable — your valid rollback layers are: the exported .reg backup and the files moved to the Recycle Bin.
confirm.tier.reversible: 🔁 Reversible — deleted items go to the Recycle Bin, or a .reg backup is taken for the registry.
confirm.tier.medium: The actions below run exactly as shown. Some are only partially reversible (via a .bak).
confirm.tier.irreversible: ❌ NOT reversible — this is permanent. You must type the confirm word to continue.
clean.title: Clean
clean.subtitle: Free up space and tidy startup — every delete is a dry-run first.
clean.junk.title: Junk & temporary files
clean.startup.title: Startup programs
clean.recycle.title: Recycle Bin
clean.recycle.confirm.title: Empty the Recycle Bin?
clean.recycle.confirm.body: This permanently deletes everything in the Recycle Bin on all drives. It cannot be undone.
clean.ext.title: Browser extensions (inventory only)
clean.ext.note: Listing only — removal isn't offered (profile/sync risk).
clean.result.title: Result
backup.title: Back up
backup.subtitle: Copy your settings and files before a format — secrets are never copied.
backup.secret.manual: Contains secrets — re-login after format (not copied)
backup.report.title: Backup report
backup.payloadOutsideRepo: The backup folder must be outside the app folder.
backup.empty.prompt: Choose a backup folder outside this app, then build the plan to preview what will be copied.
migration.title: What should we keep before formatting?
migration.subtitle: Choose what you want to carry to the new PC. Every row states honestly what will happen.
migration.promise.restore.title: ✓ What we can carry
migration.promise.restore.body: Files, settings, game saves, and a list of programs to reinstall.
migration.promise.manual.title: ✗ What needs your action
migration.promise.manual.body: Programs themselves and credentials locked to this PC require reinstall or sign-in.
migration.scan.title: Read-only scan
migration.scan.running: Reading program and profile sources. Nothing is being changed.
migration.scan.error: The scan could not finish. Nothing was changed; you can retry.
migration.scan.profile.pending: The profile is confirmed when the on-demand scan starts.
migration.groups.heading: The most critical items are first. Clear anything you do not want.
migration.group.IrreplaceablePersonal.su: Unique data that formatting could permanently remove
migration.group.SecurityIdentity.title: Passwords and accounts
migration.group.SecurityIdentity.subtitl: Machine-bound credentials require export, sync, or sign-in
migration.group.AiCli.title: AI and command-line tools
migration.group.AiCli.subtitle: Prompts, settings, memories, and tool configuration
migration.group.DevConfigEditors.title: Developer tools and editors
migration.group.DevConfigEditors.subtitl: Settings move; programs may need reinstalling
migration.group.GameSaves.title: Game saves
migration.group.GameSaves.subtitle: Local progress that may not exist in the cloud
migration.group.Browsers.title: Browsers
migration.group.Browsers.subtitle: Bookmarks and settings may move; passwords may not
migration.group.ListSettingDumps.title: Program lists and settings
migration.group.ListSettingDumps.subtitl: Inventory and configuration for rebuilding the PC
migration.group.DetectedUnrecognized.sub: Nothing is hidden; review these manually
migration.item.defaultDescription: Review this item before including it.
migration.item.forced: Selected to prevent silent data loss
migration.preview.title: Exact command preview
migration.preview.body: Preview-only strings are generated from the selection. No copy or restore runs here.
migration.manual.title: Manual steps for the new PC
migration.manual.body: Items that cannot be restored are kept in this explicit checklist.
migration.capture.title: Back up the selected items
migration.capture.destination: Choose a destination outside the app folder. The dry-run plan below is what you approve.
migration.capture.approve: I reviewed this capture plan — approve
migration.capture.outsideAppWarning: The destination must be a valid local-drive folder outside the app folder.
migration.restore.title: Restore from a migration package
migration.restore.subtitle: Choose a package from the old PC, preview the exact writes, approve the hash, then restore.
migration.restore.packageHint: The folder must contain migration-manifest.json from the capture package.
migration.restore.invalidPackageWarning: Choose valid package and checkpoint folders.
migration.restore.noManifestWarning: No migration manifest could be loaded
migration.restore.note.title: What restore can and cannot do
migration.restore.note.body: Profile-relative config files enter the write plan. Machine-locked, partial, missing, or blocked items stay in Manual for reinstall, sign-in, or hand merge.
migration.restore.planHint: The rows below are the plan tied to the hash you approve.
migration.restore.approve: I reviewed this restore plan - approve
migration.restore.skipped: Not added to the write plan
migration.restore.undo.title: .bak undo
migration.restore.undo.body: Undo replays the journaled .bak files through the same gated restore path. Missing or mismatched backups are shown as failures.
migration.restore.previewSummary: {0} writes planned · {1} skipped · {2} restored · {3} reinstall · {4} manual
migration.restore.resultSummary: {0} done · {1} failed or blocked · {2} not run · {3} restored · {4} reinstall · {5} manual
install.title: Reinstall
install.subtitle: Restore apps and settings after a format, in dependency order.
install.auth.title: Sign-in status
install.reboot.note: Some steps may need a reboot; restore resumes automatically.
install.confirm.title: Confirm — this will install software
install.confirm.body: The actions below run through the SafetyGate via winget/npm. Manual-after items are listed separately.
install.manual.title: Run these manually (login / UAC / reboot)
install.skipped.title: Skipped
install.result.title: Result
install.export.refused: Export refused: target location blocked by the safety gate
placeholder.body: This module arrives in a later build. The foundation it relies on — the SafetyGate, the typed plan engine, the audit log — is already in place and tested.
firstrun.title: Before you start
firstrun.body: Windows Care Kit is open-source and unsigned, so Windows SmartScreen may warn on first run. Every destructive action is shown as a dry-run and checked by the SafetyGate, and runs only after you explicitly approve it.
